The candidate patch doesn't address the actual regex patterns at all, operates on a different file (`src/index.ts` vs the affected `.js` files), and a 1MB limit wouldn't prevent the attack since even smaller inputs can trigger catastrophic backtracking.

The candidate patch is incomplete and speculative. It shows a partial diff removing lines from a file (parse-file.pipe.ts) that doesn't appear to exist in the actual codebase in that form. The gold patch only contains version bumps, suggesting the actual security fix was in a dependency or the real code changes weren't captured. The candidate patch doesn't address the actual vulnerability and appears to be fabricated content rather than a real fix.
